name: meloplus

metadata.name: meloplus
metadata.summary: Meloplus: Advanced python library for Melotts

documentation:
MeloPlus: Advanced Python Library for MeloTts

## 🛠️ Installation ```bash pip install meloplus ``` ## 🎙️ Usage ```python from meloplus import MeloInference # Speed is adjustable speed = 1.0 # English text = "Did you ever hear a folk tale about a giant turtle?" model = MeloInference(language="EN", device="auto") speaker_ids = model.hps.data.spk2id ``` ## 😍 Contributing ```bash pip install pre-commit pre-commit install pre-commit run --all-files ``` ## 📜 License This project is licensed under the terms of the MIT License. ## 🤗 Citation ```bibtex @software{zhao2024melo, author={Zhao, Wenliang and Yu, Xumin and Qin, Zengyi}, title = {MeloTTS: High-quality Multi-lingual Multi-accent Text-to-Speech}, url = {https://github.com/myshell-ai/MeloTTS}, year = {2023} } ```

modules.name: meloplus.app
documentation: WebUI by mrfakename <X @realmrfakename / HF @mrfakename>
Demo also available on HF Spaces: https://huggingface.co/spaces/mrfakename/MeloTTS

functions.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.cleaner.replace_symbols
documentation:
Replace symbols based on the lenguage tag.

Args:
  text:
   Input text.
  lang:
    Lenguage identifier. ex: "en", "fr", "pt", "ca".

Returns:
  The modified text
  example:
    input args:
        text: "si l'avi cau, diguem-ho"
        lang: "ca"
    Output:
        text: "si lavi cau, diguemho"

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.base.BasePhonemizer.phonemize
documentation:
Returns the `text` phonemized for the given language

Args:
    text (str):
        Text to be phonemized.

    separator (str):
        string separator used between phonemes. Default to '_'.

Returns:
    (str): Phonemized text

Base phonemizer class

Phonemization follows the following steps:
    1. Preprocessing:
        - remove empty lines
        - remove punctuation
        - keep track of punctuation marks

    2. Phonemization:
        - convert text to phonemes

    3. Postprocessing:
        - join phonemes
        - restore punctuation marks

Args:
    language (str):
        Language used by the phonemizer.

    punctuations (List[str]):
        List of punctuation marks to be preserved.

    keep_puncs (bool):
        Whether to preserve punctuation marks or not.

```

```

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.punctuation.Punctuation.strip_to_restore
documentation:
Remove punctuations from text to restore them later.

Args:

: text (str): The text to be processed.

Examples

```
"This is. example !" -> [["This is", "example"], [".", "!"]]
```

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.punctuation.Punctuation._strip_to_restore
documentation: Auxiliary method for Punctuation.preserve()

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.punctuation.Punctuation.restore
documentation:
Restore punctuation in a text.

Args:

: text (str): The text to be processed.
  puncs (List[str]): The list of punctuations map to be used for restoring.

Examples

```
['This is', 'example'], ['.', '!'] -> "This is. example!"
```

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.punctuation.Punctuation._restore
documentation: Auxiliary method for Punctuation.restore()

Handle punctuations in text.

Just strip punctuations from text or strip and restore them later.

Args:
    puncs (str): The punctuations to be processed. Defaults to `_DEF_PUNCS`.

Example:
    >>> punc = Punctuation()
    >>> punc.strip("This is. example !")
    'This is example'

    >>> text_striped, punc_map = punc.strip_to_restore("This is. example !")
    >>> ' '.join(text_striped)
    'This is example'

    >>> text_restored = punc.restore(text_striped, punc_map)
    >>> text_restored[0]
    'This is. example !'

classes.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.phonemize_gruut
documentation:
Convert input text to phonemes.

Gruut phonemizes the given `str` by seperating each phoneme character with `separator`, even for characters
that constitude a single sound.

It doesn't affect 🐸TTS since it individually converts each character to token IDs.

Examples::

: "hello how are you today?" -> `h|ɛ|l|o|ʊ| h|a|ʊ| ɑ|ɹ| j|u| t|ə|d|e|ɪ`

Args:
: text (str):

  : Text to be converted to phonemes.

  tie (bool, optional)

  : consecutive characters of a single phoneme. Else separate phoneme
    with '\_'. This option requires espeak>=1.49. Default to False.

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.is_supported_language
documentation: Returns True if `language` is supported by the backend

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.supported_languages
documentation:
Get a dictionary of supported languages.

Returns:
    List: List of language codes.

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.version
documentation:
Get the version of the used backend.

Returns:
    str: Version of the used backend.

methods.name: meloplus.text.fr_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.is_available
documentation: Return true if ESpeak is available else false

Gruut wrapper for G2P

Args:
    language (str):
        Valid language code for the used backend.

    punctuations (str):
        Characters to be treated as punctuation. Defaults to `Punctuation.default_puncs()`.

    keep_puncs (bool):
        If true, keep the punctuations after phonemization. Defaults to True.

    use_espeak_phonemes (bool):
        If true, use espeak lexicons instead of default Gruut lexicons. Defaults to False.

    keep_stress (bool):
        If true, keep the stress characters after phonemization. Defaults to False.

Example:

    >>> from TTS.tts.utils.text.phonemizers.gruut_wrapper import Gruut
    >>> phonemizer = Gruut('en-us')
    >>> phonemizer.phonemize("Be a voice, not an! echo?", separator="|")
    'b|i| ə| v|ɔ|ɪ|s, n|ɑ|t| ə|n! ɛ|k|o|ʊ?'

methods.name: meloplus.text.tone_sandhi.ToneSandhi._neural_sandhi
documentation: the meaning of jieba pos tag: https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_44174352/article/details/113731041
e.g.
word: "家里"
pos: "s"
finals: ['ia1', 'i3']

methods.name: meloplus.text.tone_sandhi.ToneSandhi._merge_bu
documentation: merge "不" and the word behind it
if don't merge, "不" sometimes appears alone according to jieba, which may occur sandhi error

methods.name: meloplus.text.tone_sandhi.ToneSandhi._merge_yi
documentation: function 1: merge "一" and reduplication words in it's left and right, e.g. "听","一","听" ->"听一听"
function 2: merge single  "一" and the word behind it
if don't merge, "一" sometimes appears alone according to jieba, which may occur sandhi error
e.g.
output seg: [['听一听', 'v']]

methods.name: meloplus.text.tone_sandhi.ToneSandhi._merge_continuous_three_tones
documentation: the first and the second words are all_tone_three

methods.name: meloplus.text.tone_sandhi.ToneSandhi._merge_continuous_three_tones_2
documentation: the last char of first word and the first char of second word is tone_three

methods.name: meloplus.text.es_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.phonemize_gruut
documentation:
Convert input text to phonemes.

Gruut phonemizes the given `str` by seperating each phoneme character with `separator`, even for characters
that constitude a single sound.

It doesn't affect 🐸TTS since it individually converts each character to token IDs.

Examples::

: "hello how are you today?" -> `h|ɛ|l|o|ʊ| h|a|ʊ| ɑ|ɹ| j|u| t|ə|d|e|ɪ`

Args:
: text (str):

  : Text to be converted to phonemes.

  tie (bool, optional)

  : consecutive characters of a single phoneme. Else separate phoneme
    with '\_'. This option requires espeak>=1.49. Default to False.

methods.name: meloplus.text.es_phonemizer.gruut_wrapper.Gruut.is_available
documentation: Return true if ESpeak is available else false

Gruut wrapper for G2P

Args:
    language (str):
        Valid language code for the used backend.

    punctuations (str):
        Characters to be treated as punctuation. Defaults to `Punctuation.default_puncs()`.

    keep_puncs (bool):
        If true, keep the punctuations after phonemization. Defaults to True.

    use_espeak_phonemes (bool):
        If true, use espeak lexicons instead of default Gruut lexicons. Defaults to False.

    keep_stress (bool):
        If true, keep the stress characters after phonemization. Defaults to False.

Example:

    >>> from TTS.tts.utils.text.phonemizers.gruut_wrapper import Gruut
    >>> phonemizer = Gruut('en-us')
    >>> phonemizer.phonemize("Be a voice, not an! echo?", separator="|")
    'b|i| ə| v|ɔ|ɪ|s, n|ɑ|t| ə|n! ɛ|k|o|ʊ?'

functions.name: meloplus.text.korean.korean_text_to_phonemes
documentation:
The input and output values look the same, but they are different in Unicode.

example :

    input = '하늘' (Unicode : 하늘), (하 + 늘)
    output = '하늘' (Unicode :하늘), (ᄒ + ᅡ + ᄂ + ᅳ + ᆯ)

functions.name: meloplus.utils.clean_checkpoints
documentation:
Freeing up space by deleting saved ckpts

Arguments:
path_to_models    --  Path to the model directory
n_ckpts_to_keep   --  Number of ckpts to keep, excluding G_0.pth and D_0.pth
sort_by_time      --  True -> chronologically delete ckpts
                      False -> lexicographically delete ckpts

Collate's training batch from normalized text, audio and speaker identities
PARAMS
------

Zero-pads model inputs and targets

classes.name: meloplus.data_utils.DistributedBucketSampler
documentation:
Maintain similar input lengths in a batch.
Length groups are specified by boundaries.
Ex) boundaries = [b1, b2, b3] -> any batch is included either {x | b1 < length(x) <=b2} or {x | b2 < length(x) <= b3}.

It removes samples which are not included in the boundaries.
Ex) boundaries = [b1, b2, b3] -> any x s.t. length(x) <= b1 or length(x) > b3 are discarded.
